items.question: For p = [0.5, 0.5] and q = [0.9, 0.1], is D_KL(P || Q) equal to D_KL(Q || P)?
options: Yes, when p and q have two outcomes
Only equal when using log base 10
items.explanation: Argument order changes the expectation; for this pair both values are finite but unequal.

items.question: Which identity links KL to cross-entropy H(P, Q) and entropy H(P)?
items.explanation: Cross-entropy exceeds entropy by exactly the KL term when P is the reference distribution.

items.question: When is D_KL(P || Q) guaranteed to be zero?
options: When P and Q are identical distributions
When p and q have the same support size
items.answer: When P and Q are identical distributions
items.explanation: Gibbs' inequality: KL ≥ 0 with equality iff P = Q (same PMF).

items.question: If q(x) = 0 where p(x) > 0 for some x, what is D_KL(P || Q)?
items.explanation: The term p log(p/q) diverges when q hits zero on support of p.
